creator: xelalex_maker@web.de
text:
Having installed the pre-built Geogebra package from Quarterly on my 10.1-RELEASE FreeBSD 10.1-RELEASE #0 r274401, it refuses to start with a bunch of error messages related to the respective shell-script:

% geogebra
FATAL: kernel too old
expr: syntax error
/usr/local/share/geogebra/geogebra/geogebra: line 349: [: -ne: unary operator expected
expr: syntax error
/usr/local/share/geogebra/geogebra/geogebra: line 369: [: -ne: unary operator expected
expr: syntax error
/usr/local/share/geogebra/geogebra/geogebra: line 372: [: -ne: unary operator expected
expr: syntax error
/usr/local/share/geogebra/geogebra/geogebra: line 375: [: -ne: unary operator expected
expr: syntax error
/usr/local/share/geogebra/geogebra/geogebra: line 378: [: -ne: unary operator expected
expr: syntax error
/usr/local/share/geogebra/geogebra/geogebra: line 381: [: -ne: unary operator expected
expr: syntax error
/usr/local/share/geogebra/geogebra/geogebra: line 384: [: -ne: unary operator expected
/usr/local/share/geogebra/geogebra/geogebra: line 432: /usr/local/share/geogebra/jre1.7.0_45-/bin/java: No such file or directory
